message:
fix(test): eliminate race in repro_race_leak by yielding winner once

The repro_race_leak test had a timing dependency: the winner task
completed immediately on first poll, but the race combinator needs
both async blocks to be polled at least once to set up their JoinFutures
before any result is available. This meant the test relied on internal
polling order rather than explicit sequencing.

Fix by making the winner task yield once via poll_fn before returning
"winner". This ensures:
1. First poll of race_future polls both async blocks (both pending)
2. Winner task is then polled to Ready
3. Second poll of race_future finds the winner and returns

Also simplify the loser_flag clone (move directly into async block)
and restructure the race polling to use explicit pin + poll steps
instead of .await, matching the test's manual executor pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
